news.headline: Hemp Beverage Alliance to host Hemp Beverage Expo in Atlanta on July 9-11, 2025, in partnership with Jage Media.
news.summary: A trade association representing the growing hemp beverage sector, the Hemp Beverage Alliance, disclosed its intention to organize the Hemp Beverage Expo from July 9 to July 11 in Atlanta, Georgia. The event, held in collaboration with Jage Media, seeks to bring together industry professionals, showcasing the latest innovations in the sector and promoting networking opportunities for the participants.

news.headline: Broad Street Development hires Matt Sobel and Ben Kahan to acquire $2B in assets, expanding investment capabilities.
news.summary: In an exciting development, prominent investor and developer Broad Street Development (BSD) is marking its 20th anniversary by announcing the addition of two new senior executives to its team. Matt Sobel and Ben Kahan are now part of the expanding investment capabilities at BSD. This move comes at a significant time for the company, showcasing its commitment to growth and innovation. With these new appointments, BSD is poised to strengthen its position in the market and drive forward its strategic objectives.

news.headline: Bipartisan bill aims to protect utilities and ratepayers from PFAS costs; supported by Water Environment Federation.
news.summary: On February 13, 2025, in Washington, the Water Environment Federation (WEF) announced their firm endorsement for the reintroduction of the Water Systems PFAS Liability Protection Act. Sponsored by Representatives Marie Gluesenkamp Perez (D-WA) and Celeste Maloy (R-UT), the bipartisan bill seeks to provide liability protection to water systems dealing with per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).

news.headline: Deere Q1 2025: Sales down 30% to $8.51B, beating $7.70B consensus. Net sales $6.809B. EPS $3.19, beating $3.14.
news.summary: Deere & Co (NYSE: DE) announced a 30% year-over-year decrease in net sales and revenue for the first quarter of 2025, totaling $8.51 billion, which surpassed the anticipated $7.70 billion. Specifically, net sales amounted to $6.809 billion for the quarter, a significant decline from the $10.486 billion recorded in 2024. The company reported an earnings per share (EPS) of $3.19, down from $6.23 in the previous year, surpassing the expected $3.14. Within its various segments, Production & Precision Agriculture net sales decreased by 37% year-over-year to $3.067 billion, while Small Agriculture & Turf net sales saw a 28% decline to $1.748 billion. Furthermore, Construction & Forestry revenue dropped by 38% to $1.994 billion. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: CVS Health reported Q4 sales of $97.71B, exceeding consensus of $97.19B. Adjusted EPS at $1.19 ($2.12 prior year) beat consensus $0.93. Analysts raise forecasts.
news.summary: CVS Health Corp (NYSE:CVS) announced positive results for the fourth quarter. The company achieved sales of $97.71 billion, surpassing the expected $97.19 billion. Despite a decrease in adjusted EPS from $2.12 to $1.19 compared to the previous year, the figure exceeded the estimated $0.93. The decline was mainly attributed to challenges in the Health Care Benefits segment, influenced by ongoing utilization pressure and the adverse effects of the Company’s Medicare Advantage star ratings for the 2024 payment year. CVS emphasized its capacity to provide a streamlined, interconnected experience that enhances efficiency, reduces costs, and promotes well-being. The Pharmacy and Consumer Wellness division experienced growth, underscoring the success in key areas of the business.

The latest model has generated significant interest and is now on sale at the dealership. This announcement was made on February 13, 2025. The dealership invites customers to experience the rugged qualities of the Ford Bronco firsthand.

news.headline: Crispy Green's top-selling freeze-dried fruit snack now in nationwide Whole Foods Market stores.
news.summary: Crispy Green®, based in FAIRFIELD, N.J., revealed on Feb. 13, 2025, that their popular freeze-dried fruit snack, Crispy Fruit™, is now available in convenient 4-PACKS. The company, known for producing the top-selling freeze-dried fruit snack in the U.S., introduced this new packaging for their two best-selling Crispy Fruit snacks. Families seeking a delicious and nutritious snack option will appreciate these convenient packs, which aim to cater to the needs of active households.

news.headline: Sprinturf's Healthy Spaces Council meeting in Chattanooga, TN gathers architects and professionals to advance recreation and play design.
news.summary: On February 13, 2025, in Daniel Island, South Carolina, a group of prominent architects and professionals specializing in recreation and play area design gathered at the Healthy Spaces Council meeting. The conference took place in Chattanooga, TN and was organized by Sprinturf, a subsidiary of PlayCore. The event aimed to discuss innovative approaches to creating engaging play spaces.

These pops are designed to cater to active, growing kids and families who prioritize health. The company, based in San Diego, made the official announcement on February 13, 2025. The initiative aims to provide a nutritious and delicious snack option.

Mink Ventures finds nickel-copper-cobalt mineralization at Warren, with disseminated and semi-massive sulphides in every hole.
news.summary: Disseminated and semi-massive sulphides were found in all drill holes at the Mink site, confirming the accuracy of geophysical data. This significant discovery indicates a high likelihood of valuable mineral deposits in the area.

news.headline: NSBE celebrates 50th anniversary at annual convention in Chicago with expected record-breaking attendance amid federal DEI pushback.
news.summary: With diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives encountering resistance from the federal government, the National Society of Black Engineers® (NSBE®) foresees a surge in attendance for its upcoming event in Chicago. The organization, headquartered in Alexandria, Virginia, is anticipating a record-breaking number of participants at the event scheduled for February 13, 2025. NSBE, known as one of the leading student-led organizations in the country, is enthusiastic about the increasing interest in the event amidst the current challenges to DEI efforts nationwide.

news.headline: US wholesale inflation in January 2025 surpasses expectations, driven by increased food and energy prices.
news.summary: In recent statistics, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the country has reached its lowest point in five years, dropping to 4.2%. This decline represents a significant decrease of 1.5% compared to the previous year. Additionally, the economy has shown strong growth with a GDP increase of 3.8% in the last quarter. These positive indicators point towards a recovering job market and a strengthening economy. This news comes as a welcome relief to many individuals and businesses struggling in the aftermath of the recent economic downturn.

news.headline: Bio-IT World Venture, Innovation & Partnering Conference in Boston on April 3, 2025 with a focus on AI. Participants access 24th annual Bio-IT World Conference & Expo from April 2-4.
news.summary: Attendees will have the opportunity to attend the 24th annual Bio-IT World Conference & Expo from April 2-4 in Boston. The event is organized by Cambridge Healthtech Institute. The conference, known as Bio-IT World 2025, is set to attract a global audience.

news.headline: Barclay Group delivers 7 new Kroger-anchored shopping centers in 15 months, expanding their portfolio.
news.summary: Barclay Group, a well-known real estate development firm, has recently enhanced its portfolio by acquiring long-term operating assets in Phoenix. As part of its retail development strategy, the company has successfully added seven Kroger-anchored locations to its portfolio. This expansion underscores Barclay Group's commitment to growth and diversification, highlighting their strategic vision in the real estate market. The move aligns with their goal of bolstering their presence in the retail sector by investing in established, high-performing assets.

news.headline: Basis Industrial buys 142,069 sq ft property in Orlando for $20M, making them a major landlord in the area.
news.summary: Acquisition Positions Basis as Major Landholder in Orlando Central ParkAs of February 13, 2025, Basis Industrial, a Delray Beach-headquartered real estate company, recently finalized a purchase that solidifies its standing as one of the principal landowners in Orlando Central Park. This privately-owned firm, known for its comprehensive approach to real estate, now boasts a substantial portfolio in the region, with an Orlando office overseeing operations.

This particular entry choice enables participants to compete in numerous awards categories within the event.

news.headline: Rev: Ithaca Startup Works offers summer-long program for entrepreneurs to develop physical products for commercial viability.

This program runs throughout the summer and is designed to assist entrepreneurs in refining physical product ideas to evaluate their market viability.

news.headline: COEPTIS launches COEP Venture Group investing in AI, RPA, AI Agent Startups. Nasdaq: COEP. Dedicated to cutting-edge tech.
news.summary: On February 13, 2025, in Wexford, Pennsylvania, COEPTIS, Inc. (Nasdaq: COEP) introduced the COEP Venture Group. This new investment arm aims to support and boost technology startups focused on Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), and AI Agents. COEPTIS, Inc. is a tech company committed to advancing cutting-edge technologies and artificial intelligence in biotechnology therapeutic solutions. The venture group will provide funding and resources for startups in these sectors, solidifying COEPTIS' position at the forefront of innovation in technology investment.

news.headline: Interest-rate futures gain, reflecting rising bets on a Fed rate cut in July, but a September rate cut is still seen as more likely.

ASEAN-Japan Women Entrepreneurs’ Summit in Kuala Lumpur focuses on driving innovation, sustainability, and inclusivity. Important organizations involved: AJC, ASEAN Youth Organization, ACCMSME, Women Leadership Foundation Malaysia.
news.summary: In Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, the ASEAN-Japan Centre (AJC) partnered with the ASEAN Youth Organization, supported by the ASEAN Coordinating Committee for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (ACCMSME) and Women Leadership Foundation Malaysia, to host the ASEAN-Japan Women Entrepreneurs’ Summit. This summit highlighted the importance of women entrepreneurs in promoting economic innovation, sustainability, and inclusivity within the ASEAN-Japan region. The event saw the participation of key stakeholders, with a focus on empowering women in business.

news.headline: Viz.ai's Stroke Solution proven to improve patient outcomes and hospital economics according to new studies at ISC 2025.
news.summary: In San Francisco, Viz.ai, a prominent provider of AI-driven disease detection and care coordination solutions, disclosed recent clinical findings endorsing progress in neurovascular treatment. At the International Stroke Conference (ISC) 2025, two research reports highlighted the favorable results of utilizing Viz.ai technology in treating patients with acute large vessel occlusion (LVO) strokes. The studies demonstrated the tangible benefits of Viz.ai in real-world medical settings, showcasing its potential to significantly impact outcomes for individuals with LVO strokes, a major contributor to mortality and disability rates in the United States.

news.headline: Communication services sector and industry on the rise. Stay updated with Bloomberg for latest news.
Himax Q4 revenue grows 4.2% YoY to $237.22M, earnings per ADS $0.14 beats estimate, gross margin 30.5%, stock price rises.
news.summary: Himax Technologies, Inc posted a fiscal fourth-quarter revenue growth of 4.2% YoY, reaching $237.22 million, surpassing the analyst consensus of $221.09 million. Net revenues from the Arm Holdings customer rose by 6.7% Q/Q due to increased orders. Earnings per ADS stood at $0.14, higher than the analyst estimate of $0.10. The stock price saw an increase following the report. The gross margin hit 30.5%, surpassing guidance and previous quarters. Revenue from large display drivers reached $25.0 million. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Round Rock Healthy Hearts at Stony Point High School wins HOSA Community Awareness title out of 35 teams, reaching 300,000+ for Heart Health Initiative.
news.summary: In Round Rock, Texas, on February 13, 2025, a group known as Round Rock Healthy Hearts, comprising students from Stony Point High School, achieved top honors among 35 competing teams in the Health Occupations Students of America (HOSA) Area 1 Community Awareness contest held at Byron P. Steele High School.

Metalpha specializes in digital asset wealth management and is recognized as a key player in the global market. news.headline: Carrier CEO David Gitlin will present at Citi's 2025 Global Industrial Tech and Mobility Conference on Feb 20, 2025 at 1:50pm ET.

This event will be streamed live, and viewers can tune in to hear more about Carrier Global Corporation (NYSE: CARR) and its latest insights in the industry.

news.headline: Points4Purpose launches new cashback retailer rewards to facilitate micro-donations to charities, revolutionizing loyalty rewards.
news.summary: Miami, February 13, 2025 - Points4Purpose, Inc. has made a significant announcement regarding its plan to transform the loyalty rewards sector and revolutionize philanthropic contributions in the United States. The company has revealed its latest initiative, a cashback rewards system along with an automated micro-philanthropy platform. This innovative move aims to enhance the process of providing support to charitable causes by seamlessly integrating it with consumers' everyday transactions. The platform promises to streamline the donation process and increase contributions to various charitable organizations across the country.

news.headline: In November 2024, increase in acute respiratory diseases and infections. Hypertension rises nationally to fourth position. Telehealth utilization decreases nationally but increases in West and Midwest.
news.summary: In the latest data released on telehealth diagnostics, hypertension has moved up from fifth to fourth in the list of top five categories nationally. Interestingly, while telehealth utilization saw a decline across the nation, as well as in the South and Northeast regions, it increased in the West and Midwest. This shift suggests a pattern of changing health concerns and preferences among Americans. This update comes in the midst of various healthcare trends, with hypertension standing out as a significant health issue.

(ATS), a reputable provider of industrial maintenance and reliability solutions, has unveiled a groundbreaking offer. They have rolled out a new guarantee which promises customers a significant three times return on investment (ROI) for their Reliability 360® Machine Health Monitoring service. This offer is now accessible to all interested parties.
